findings.title: Stack buffer overflow in {{function|cgi_handler}}()
findings.summary: Unbounded copy of attacker-controlled input into a fixed stack buffer in {{function|cgi_handler}} allows overwriting the saved return address.
findings.reasoning: After decompiling {{function|cgi_handler}} and reading the imports, the body copies the request 'token' field into a 1040-byte stack buffer using {{sink|strcpy}} with no length check. The source is network-reachable and pre-auth; recon shows no stack canary, so the saved return address is controllable.
